key_insight: The hysteretic behavior in the acoustic response of NiTiCu and Parylene C composite rods reveals temperature-dependent phononic spectra.
engineering_relevance: This study provides insights into designing temperature-sensitive materials for applications such as sensors and actuators.
method: Utilized the transfer-matrix method to compute Bloch band structures.
Analyzed the transmission spectrum of finite composite rods.
Examined the impact of geometric filling fraction on spectral hysteresis.
evidence_signal: Acoustic response across the martensitic transformation shows distinct phase fractions and phononic spectra based on thermal history.
watch_item: The dependence on thermal history may complicate real-time applications and predictability in dynamic environments.
paper_title: Hysteretic Acoustic Band Structures in Shape-Memory Composite Thin Rods
